metaBuilds.title: Physical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 3,053 effective HP vs magical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 0% of our best core on magical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Physical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 4,127 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Lethality Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 871 damage in one combo in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Sparrow ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 39% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Murdock deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 39% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Eden ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 37% of its bite; you give up 39% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Drongo deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 39% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Revenant ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 39% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. TwinBlast ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 39% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 39% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 28 — about 3,053 effective HP vs magical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +60% more magical survivability: try Stonewall over Soulbinder (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Physical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 28 — about 4,127 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.7× the physical survivability: try Stonewall over Soulbinder (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Lethality Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 28 — about 871 damage in one combo in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
